creator: lightside@gmx.com
text:
(In reply to comment #3)
> I'll take it, since it is addressed to me.
Thanks for fast response.

(In reply to comment #3)
> But, for future reference, I prefer patches that are rooted at ports/,
> e.g, cd blah/ports; svn diff games/oolite > diff.out .  Thanks.
Does it relates to how patches tested on some kind of armv* machine? If so, then there is some intermediate solution:
% cat oolite.diff | sed -E 's|^[-+]{3} |&games/oolite/| ; s|^Index: |&games/oolite/|' > games_oolite.diff

Personally, I created patches with using following command(s):
% svn co https://svn.FreeBSD.org/ports/head/games/oolite
% cd oolite
Do some changes inside oolite directory and create a patch:
% svn diff --ignore-properties > ../oolite.diff

This is similar to how it was explained in (current) FreeBSD Porter's Handbook:
https://www.freebsd.org/doc/en/books/porters-handbook/port-upgrading.html#svn-diff

But I understand, if there are some complications.
Previously, I just created patches with using diff command (also explained in chapter 11 of FreeBSD Porter's Handbook), e.g.
% diff -ruN oolite.orig oolite > oolite.diff
before some PR's creator/submitter (privately) mentioned about svn diff, in response to attached patch in bug 219608.
